title: dianthus divaricatus
intro: Dianthus divaricatus, also known as the fringed pink or fragrant pink, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Dianthus divaricatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate cold symptoms, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and topical creams for various health applications.
